message:
fix: accept all bead prefixes in gt cat and clean up on move failure (gt-2q24f)

- cat.go: Replace hardcoded prefix list (bd-, hq-, mol-) with pattern-based
  validation that accepts any lowercase-letter prefix followed by a dash.
  This fixes gt cat rejecting valid gt-, wisp-, dolt-, sky-, wy- prefixed IDs.

- bead.go: Actually attempt to close the new bead when source close fails
  during move, instead of just printing a warning about split-brain state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,39 @@
+package cmd
+
+import "testing"
+
+func TestIsBeadID(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		input string
+		want  bool
+	}{
+		// Valid bead IDs - all known prefixes
+		{"gt-abc123", true},
+		{"bd-xyz", true},
+		{"hq-foo", true},
+		{"mol-bar", true},
+		{"wisp-baz", true},
+		{"dolt-qux", true},
+		{"sky-abc", true},
+		{"wy-def", true},
+		// Multi-segment prefixes
+		{"hq-cv-foo", true},
+		// Invalid inputs
+		{"", false},
+		{"-abc", false},
+		{"abc", false},
+		{"ABC-123", false},
+		{"123-abc", false},
+		{"gt-", false},
+		{"-", false},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.input, func(t *testing.T) {
+			got := isBeadID(tt.input)
+			if got != tt.want {
+				t.Errorf("isBeadID(%q) = %v, want %v", tt.input, got, tt.want)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
